name: Sant Feliu de Llobregat, Can Parellada, Castellciuró, Puig d'Olorda, Santa Creu d'Olorda – Circular Route
name_original: Sant Feliu de Llobregat, Can Parellada, Castellciuró, Puig d'Olorda, Santa Creu d'Olorda – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Feliu De Llobregat offers access to diverse landscapes at the foot of the Collserola mountain range. The region features Mediterranean forests of pine and holm oak, with trails providing views of Barcelona and natural springs. Proximity to the Llobregat Delta offers wetland environments, while the Garraf Nature Park presents karst landscapes with unique rock formations. Best hiking trails around Sant Feliu De Llobregat The most popular hiking route is Sant Feliu de Llobregat, Can Parellada, Castellciuró, Puig d'Olorda, Santa Creu d'Olorda – Circular Route, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Collserola hills, passing through historical sites and offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Can Parellada Valley – Fornot de les Torres Lime Kiln loop from Sant Feliu de Llobregat, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.6 km) path. This trail winds through the Can Parellada Valley, featuring natural scenery and historical lime kilns. Local hikers also love the Penya del Moro Viewpoint – Giant Wooden Bench loop from Sant Feliu de Llobregat, a 4.4 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through wooded areas to a notable viewpoint, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Sant Feliu De Llobregat is defined by Mediterranean forests, access to the Collserola mountain range, and varied terrain including hills and valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Sant Feliu De Llobregat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 750 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Feliu De Llobregat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sant Feliu De Llobregat?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails around Sant Feliu De Llobregat, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find options for all skill levels, including 15 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 6 more challenging trails.

Q: What are the general characteristics of hiking trails in Sant Feliu De Llobregat?
A: The trails in Sant Feliu De Llobregat are characterized by their diverse Mediterranean landscapes, ranging from lush pine and holm oak forests within the Collserola Natural Park to unique karst formations in the nearby Garraf Nature Park. Many routes offer panoramic views of Barcelona and the surrounding natural scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Feliu De Llobregat?
A: Sant Feliu De Llobregat offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Even in winter, the climate is generally mild, making it suitable for hiking, though summer can be quite warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Sant Feliu De Llobregat?
A: Yes, there are 15 easy hiking routes around Sant Feliu De Llobregat, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails often feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, such as those found in local parks or along the Llobregat Delta.

Q: Can I find trails with viewpoints in Sant Feliu De Llobregat?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich with viewpoints offering spectacular vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23828061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya del Moro Viewpoint – Giant Wooden Bench loop from Sant Feliu de Llobregat&lt;/a&gt; leads to a notable viewpoint. Other spots like the Sant Elm hermitage viewpoint provide sweeping views from Tossa to Begur and from the sea to the mountains.

Q: Are there any natural attractions like caves or waterfalls to explore?
A: Yes, the area around Sant Feliu De Llobregat features several interesting natural attractions. You can explore caves such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Juanita Mine&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt;. For a scenic waterfall, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2765506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Grotto in Parc de Torreblanca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Sant Feliu De Llobregat dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Collserola Natural Park and surrounding areas are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy your hike with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Sant Feliu De Llobregat?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951838435/sant-feliu-de-llobregat-can-parellada-castellciuro-puig-d-olorda-santa-creu-d-olorda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Feliu de Llobregat, Can Parellada, Castellciuró, Puig d'Olorda, Santa Creu d'Olorda – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Collserola hills.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Sant Feliu De Llobregat?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse Mediterranean forests, the accessibility to the Collserola mountain range, and the varied terrain that offers options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some trails offer views of or pass near water bodies. The Collserola Natural Park, which is easily accessible from Sant Feliu de Llobregat, includes the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4482632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallvidrera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing a peaceful natural setting for walks.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails using public transport?
A: Sant Feliu De Llobregat is well-connected by public transport, making many trailheads accessible. Local buses and trains can take you to various points around the Collserola Natural Park and other starting locations. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for specific routes to plan your journey.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region's trails often weave through areas with historical significance. Within Collserola Natural Park, you can discover remnants of Iberian settlements and Gothic castles. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23838036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Can Parellada Valley – Fornot de les Torres Lime Kiln loop from Sant Feliu de Llobregat&lt;/a&gt;, even feature historical lime kilns.
tips: If you have in mind to do a route through the Molins de Rei area, do not forget to visit the Castellciuró, you will find it in the direction of Santa Creu L'Orde, ancient ruins that still stand contemplating the Baix Llobregat. Easy access with possibilities to follow other directions from there.
Can Vilagut is a work by Molins de Rei (Baix Llobregat) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.
An extremely interesting industrial ruin. You don't get to see that often.
Puig d'Olorda is a 450-meter peak located between the municipalities of Barcelona, Molins de Rei and Sant Feliu de Llobregat, in the Collserola mountains.
La Pedrera dels Ocells in the municipality of Barcelona is a viewpoint located in the Collserola mountain range near the Puig d'Olorda and the hermitage of Santa Creu d'Olorda.

In this area you have a romantic chapel, a restaurant and a picknick area.
The chapel is only open on Saturdays from 11 to 13 o'clock. The restaurant and picknick area are perfect for a stop when hiking through the Collserola park. The restaurant might seem a bit expensive, but for being in the middle of the park it's okay.

The Masiá Can Parellada, is originally from the 12th century with extensions in the 18th century. The farmhouses are typical isolated Catalan constructions basically dedicated to the cultivation of fields, carob, vineyard, almond and fruit trees, in them there are also farm and working animals, such as donkeys, chickens, goats, sheep. etc etc...
A beautiful farmhouse along this route, outside you will find a swing and a large esplanade where you can make a stop, to eat or simply rest.
